pages.text: The $(item)Village Center Skyseed$() — $(item)5 emeralds$(), an iron ingot, planks and cobblestone. The grand village: a bigger Trade Post on a huge island, with a guardian iron golem.$(br2)$(bold)Where to look$()$(br)Emeralds and iron both come off a $(item)Rocky$() isle (throw it $(bold)high$() for emeralds).
Everything's in hand — here's how it goes together.

The premium village island: the same street village as a Trade Post, but bigger in every way — a $(bold)huge$() island, a deeper street network, and a guaranteed $(bold)4 to 6$() shops, with an iron golem on guard. One throw, a whole settled village.

$(bold)A bigger Trade Post$()$(br)Streets branch off a central square, lined with profession cottages — farmer, librarian, fisherman, fletcher — and larger landmark builds: a blacksmith's $(item)forge$() and an open meeting hall with a $(item)bell$(). Fields, gardens and lamp-lit plazas fill the gaps; the huge island is kept a wide plateau, not a deep cone.
Each villager has a bed and a job site, so the village fills with trades on its own. The $(item)iron golem$() guards them — raids are disabled on Skyseed worlds, but mobs at night are not.

$(bold)A late-game reward$()$(br)Costs $(item)5 emeralds$(), an iron ingot, planks and cobblestone. You'll want an established Rocky or Ancient mining run before this is within reach — but it pays back the whole economy at once.
